visual.notes: Dark Stripe/Linear/Anthropic DNA preserved. Telemetry/console rhythm executed via JetBrains Mono numerals, dotted top borders on console-cards, ASCII section dividers (═══, ▍▍▍), CRT shimmer overlay on hero, blinking caret, evidence code-blocks with > prompts and gutter line numbers. Aurora blurs tighter & more concentrated. Indigo→Mango gradient text/accents and grid backgrounds carried over with slightly more saturated panel borders. Dashboard-like /insights table is the strongest visual differentiator from v7c.
strengths: console-card dotted top border + // LAYER 01 mono header reads as console chrome, not a generic feature card
stat-readouts (key=val) feel like terminal output without sacrificing legibility
AUC gauge + microplan grid retained from v7c sit naturally in the operator-console aesthetic
ASCII dividers (═══════ and ▍▍▍▍) provide rhythm without adding chrome
weaknesses: CRT shimmer is intentionally subtle but may not survive aggressive lossy screenshot compression
stat-readout density on /verify is high; readers may need a moment to parse three readouts in a row

brand.notes: Indigo (#3843D0) + Mango (#FC5F36) + dark navy (#0A0620) palette preserved exactly. Work Sans 200/600 retained for headlines; mono is a typographic complement (numerals, run-IDs, anchor-IDs, console chrome only) not a brand departure. Brand spirit (technical product, polish, evidence-led) is amplified, not undermined.

typography_check.work_sans_use: all headlines, body copy, lede, audience cards, narrative blocks, learning sub-headers
typography_check.mono_stack: JetBrains Mono → IBM Plex Mono → SF Mono → Menlo → Consolas
typography_check.mono_use_scope: numerals (.m-num, .s-num, .mx-num, .num, .l-num, stat-readout, AUC gauge), run-IDs, anchor IDs, eyebrows (with // prefix), console-card heads, footer build-stamps, insights table headers + scope pills + ts-cells, methodology footer body, evidence-block code

strengths: Mono never invades headline weight or body copy — only telemetry/numeric surfaces
Indigo→Mango gradient text appears in hero italic, learning numerals, AUC gauge, impact stats — the same hierarchy as v7c
// prefix as a subtle visual signature ties console aesthetic into eyebrows and section heads without adding chrome

content.notes: Every above-threshold nugget routed per IA Content Routing table. LDVP step pages each carry 3 learning sections in 250-600 word range using Pattern D timeline. Program pages carry 3-4 learnings in Pattern B two-column long-form. /insights renders 20 above-threshold nuggets in monitoring-dashboard table grouped by scope (CHC, ECD, KMC, Platform). Open Questions blocks present on all 8 depth pages (4 LDVP + 4 program) with verbatim entries from IA Open Questions Roster.

topics: platform portability into a new domain
what we expect to learn from first cohort

strengths: Every numeric claim is scoped (scope=chc, scope=ecd.malawi.n50) — no platform-wide rebadging
Open Questions not invented; each entry traces to IA Roster
Failure modes (CAR LLO dropout, microplans fail in dispersed settlements, ECD autonomy at 47%) are surfaced inside learning sections and again in Open Questions, not hidden
/insights monitoring-table format is unique to v10b and renders 20 nuggets with scope, tier, run-ID, and per-row open question

responsive.notes: Same media-query strategy as v7c, extended for new console-grid, longform two-column, trail-grid, and insights-table. Mobile breakpoints: 980px (tablet) and 580px (phone). Dropdown nav collapses to flat hamburger overlay. Insights table goes horizontal-scroll on phone. ASCII dividers truncate cleanly via overflow:hidden + white-space:nowrap.
weaknesses: Insights table uses horizontal scroll on phone (display:block; overflow-x:auto) — readable, but a more responsive card collapse would be ideal in a future iteration

code.notes: Single shared styles.css across 11 pages (~1,860 lines). Pure CSS variables for theming. No build step required. No external JS dependencies. One inline script per page (mobile-nav toggle, ~5 lines). Semantic HTML throughout: <header>, <main>-equivalent <section>, <footer>, <nav>, <article>, <table>, <dl>. All images have alt text. All pages have viewport meta. Reveal class defaults to visible; JS-progressive enhancement only.
accessibility_notes: Reveal class defaults to opacity:1 / transform:none — no JS required for content visibility

depth.notes: Median depth_sharpness across 32 nugget renders = 9. Top 3 nuggets (N1 adversarial, N6 ECD attitudes, N15 displacement survey) rendered verbatim with their numbers, scope, and caveats. Pattern D treatment on LDVP pages and Pattern B treatment on program pages produce 250-600 word learning sections that satisfy the substance rule. /insights monitoring table provides a buffet view that links every nugget to its scope, tier, source, and open question.

build_warnings: Insights monitoring table on phone uses horizontal scroll (display:block; overflow-x:auto) — works, but a true card-collapse would be a future improvement.
CRT shimmer overlay (.crt) is intentionally faint (~0.025 alpha); aggressive lossy compression in screenshots may erase it. Visual experience on screen is intact.
Three program pages (KMC, ECD, Readers) use mailto/Drive resource cards with placeholder href=# for non-public source artifacts — would link to live URLs when public anchors exist.

preserved: Work Sans 200/400/600 typography for body and headlines
spun: Added JetBrains Mono for ALL numerals, run-IDs, anchor-IDs, stat readouts, and console chrome
Replaced standard pillar cards with console-data-cards (dotted top border, // LAYER 01 mono head)
Added evidence-block panels with > prompts, src/path notation, gutter line numbers
Added stat-readout pattern (key=val with mono coloration)
Added blinking-caret animation on hero h1
/insights rebuilt as a monitoring-dashboard table (vs v7c which has no /insights page)
Footer adds build-stamp line in mono (build=v10b // run_id=...)
Trail teasers homepage cards have mono tag header and right-edge mono ID badge
comparison_to_v7c.result: Same brand DNA as v7c. Different reading rhythm — Vercel dashboard meets Linear marketing meets SOC operator console. Brand score (9.4) is high because the palette and Work Sans are preserved verbatim; mono is additive, not substitutive.
